section_name: Duty on Striking Structure, Fixture, or Highway Landscaping
text:
(a) The operator of a vehicle involved in a collision resulting only in damage to a structure adjacent to a highway or a fixture or landscaping legally on or adjacent to a highway shall:

(1) take reasonable steps to locate and notify the owner or person in charge of the property of the collision and of the operator’s name and address and the registration number of the vehicle the operator was driving; and

(2) if requested and available, show the operator’s driver’s license to the owner or person in charge of the property.

(b) A person commits an offense if the person violates Subsection (a). An offense under this section is:

(1) a Class C misdemeanor, if the damage to all fixtures and landscaping is less than $200; or

(2) a Class B misdemeanor, if the damage to all fixtures and landscaping is $200 or more.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 550. Collisions & Collision Reports
##### § 550.025 — Duty on Striking Structure, Fixture, or Highway Landscaping

(a) The operator of a vehicle involved in a collision resulting only in damage to a structure adjacent to a highway or a fixture or landscaping legally on or adjacent to a highway shall:

    (1) take reasonable steps to locate and notify the owner or person in charge of the property of the collision and of the operator’s name and address and the registration number of the vehicle the operator was driving; and

    (2) if requested and available, show the operator’s driver’s license to the owner or person in charge of the property.

(b) A person commits an offense if the person violates Subsection (a). An offense under this section is:

    (1) a Class C misdemeanor, if the damage to all fixtures and landscaping is less than $200; or

    (2) a Class B misdemeanor, if the damage to all fixtures and landscaping is $200 or more.

*Acts 2023, 88th Leg., R.S., Ch. 709 (H.B.2190), Sec. 49, eff. September 1, 2023.*

subsections.label: a

take reasonable steps to locate and notify the owner or person in charge of the property of the collision and of the operator’s name and address and the registration number of the vehicle the operator was driving; and
if requested and available, show the operator’s driver’s license to the owner or person in charge of the property.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: A person commits an offense if the person violates Subsection (a). An offense under this section is:
a Class C misdemeanor, if the damage to all fixtures and landscaping is less than $200; or
a Class B misdemeanor, if the damage to all fixtures and landscaping is $200 or more.
